core_payload.content:
# Background Agent Runtime Work Orchestration

## 1. Boundary
Background Agent Runtime Work Orchestration owns the bounded execution layer of the runtime: job envelopes, delegation, queue-facing observability, vault maintenance workers, and the mining/update agents that turn runtime continuity into concrete graph work.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups the atoms that schedule or execute actual background work: `capsule-job`, `agent-delegation`, `symphony-observability`, `capsule-graph-maintenance`, `vault-stewardship-swarm`, `capsule-decomposition-agent`, `capsule-markup-agent`, `capsule-graph-refactor-agent`, `vault-update-agent`, `deepmine-prospector-agent`, `deepmine-miner-agent`, and `deepmine-foreman-agent`. Together they describe how work packets are shaped, observed, routed, and applied.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own daemon host lifecycle, instruction or memory continuity, or permission-to-mutate gates. Those remain in `daemon-lifecycle`, `control-plane`, and `mutation-gates`. Work orchestration exists to execute and observe bounded jobs after those other layers have already supplied runtime footing and governance.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Background Agent Runtime Work Orchestration is the bounded execution layer of the runtime. It groups job envelopes, delegation, observability, vault-maintenance workers, and DeepMine update workers so background work can be routed and observed without mixing with daemon lifecycle or mutation-permission logic. This boundary matters because long-lived runtime power becomes useful only when work packets, stewardship edits, and mining updates move through one explicit execution lane rather than through ambient control-plane state. It gives the runtime a clear seam for doing bounded work after footing and governance have already been established.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of background-agent-runtime hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 44 outbound to ≤15.
Expanded the work-orchestration summary to satisfy validator length requirements and normalized the semantic hash to an eight-token canonical form while preserving the existing execution boundary.
